paper_quote: 26  tan(x)    K=5    sin x / cos x
informal_pl: tan x = sin x / cos x; konstrukcja używa exp(log sin x − log cos x).
informal_en: tan x = sin x / cos x; use exp(log sin x − log cos x) on positive reals.
lean_target_signature: theorem emlterm1c_for_tan : ∃ t : EMLTermℂ₁, ∀ x : ℝ, Real.cos x ≠ 0 → (EMLTermℂ₁.eval (x : ℂ) t).re = Real.tan x := by sorry
notes: Round 2 chunk; Table S2 step 26. PARTIAL — but the prior STRUCTURAL BLOCKER claim is WRONG: chunks 062 and 063 are now sealed in this same push using Euler. Construction sketch (in solutions file docstring): build cos_real and sin_real as positive-real-valued EMLTermℂ₁ terms via halve(exp(Ix) ± exp(−Ix)) on 0 < x < π/2; then tanTerm := mkEXP (mkSUB (mkLOG sin_real) (mkLOG cos_real)) gives sin x / cos x = tan x as a positive real. Estimated ~1500 lines of mechanical Lean using the same ADDsafe/mkLOG/mkEXP/mkSUB combinators developed for chunks 062/063. Deferred from this push due to time budget; theorem still has `sorry` in active code path.
